explicit_text: My parents were born in Bhutan, but they had to leave in 1992 because of the civil war. The government there did not allow Nepali people to stay, so they moved to Nepal. Before they left Bhutan, my parents lived near each other and later got married in 1995.
implicit_text: My parents were born in a small Himalayan kingdom, but they had to leave in 1992 because of the civil war. The government there did not allow Nepali people to stay, so they moved to Nepal. Before they left that kingdom, my parents lived near each other and later got married in 1995.

explicit_text: I was born in a refugee camp in Nepal in 1997 after my parents fled Bhutan. They first stayed in the forest before the Nepalese government allowed them to build bamboo houses and provided food supplies. Later, when we left the camp, we flew from Nepal’s capital, Kathmandu, on our way to the United States.

explicit_text: In 2014, my family applied for resettlement in the United States because our relatives told us we could have a better life there.
